pages.name: Watch: Galápagos Finch Evolution (Ch. 22)
pages.description: HHMI video on natural selection & speciation in Darwin's finches, with clickable chapter markers and a downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch: DNA Sequence Data & Models of Evolution (Ch. 23)
pages.description: Presentation on molecular systematics, homology, parsimony & substitution models, with clickable chapter markers and a downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch: The Phylogenetic Tree of Anole Lizards (Ch. 23)
pages.description: HHMI video on anole adaptation, speciation & building a phylogenetic tree from DNA, with clickable chapter markers and a downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch (Supplemental): The Logic of Phylogenetics & Tree Construction (Ch. 23)
pages.description: Supplemental deep-dive on the history and logic of phylogenetic trees (homology, homoplasy, parsimony); optional past ~22:30. Clickable chapter markers and a downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch: Diversity of Protists (Ch. 28)
pages.description: Biology viewing resource covering protist diversity: euglenids, diatoms, amoebas, green algae, parasites (Plasmodium, Trypanosoma), and ciliates. Clickable chapter markers and a downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Asset Test
pages.description: Internal asset/style test page - not for Blackboard

pages.name: Watch: The Secret Behind Bioluminescent Bays (Ch. 28)
pages.description: SciShow video on dinoflagellate bioluminescence: protist classification, luciferin/luciferase biochemistry, defense mechanisms, saxitoxin toxicity, and the ecology of bioluminescent bays. Clickable chapter markers and downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch: Fungi (Ch. 31)
pages.description: Bozeman Science overview of fungi: phylogeny, shared characteristics (chitin, hyphae, heterotrophy), reproduction, and the five major phyla (Ascomycota, Basidiomycota, Chytridiomycota, Glomeromycota, Zygomycota). Clickable chapter markers and downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch: Plants (Ch. 29)
pages.description: Bozeman Science overview of land plant evolution: phylogeny, shared characteristics (cellulose, embryophytes, alternation of generations), four evolutionary milestones (bryophytes, ferns, gymnosperms, angiosperms), and photosynthesis. Clickable chapter markers and downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch: Angiosperms — Flowering Plants (Ch. 30)
pages.description: Overview of angiosperm reproduction: flower anatomy (sepals, petals, stamens, pistil), pollination strategies, double fertilization, endosperm, fruit formation, and seed dispersal. Clickable chapter markers and downloadable transcript.

pages.name: Watch: Seedless, Nonvascular Plants (Ch. 29)
pages.description: Recorded course lecture on seedless, nonvascular plants: mosses, rhizoids, alternation of generations, water-dependent fertilization, dependent sporophytes, and gametophyte dominance.

pages.name: Watch: Seedless, Vascular Plants (Ch. 29)
pages.description: Recorded course lecture on seedless vascular plants: xylem and phloem, ferns, Carboniferous forests, alternation of generations, prothalli, water-dependent fertilization, and independent sporophytes.

pages.name: Watch: Seed Plants — Gymnosperms (Ch. 30)
pages.description: Recorded course lecture on gymnosperm seed plants: shared characteristics of seed plants, seeds, naked seeds, Phylum Coniferophyta, male/female cones, sporophyte dominance, megaspore-to-egg pathway, pollen production, water-free fertilization, and seed development.

pages.name: Watch: Seed Plants — Angiosperms (Ch. 30)
pages.description: Recorded course lecture on angiosperm seed plants: flower anatomy (stamen, carpel), ovule-to-seed and ovary-to-fruit conversions, pollen tube, double fertilization, fruit types and dispersal, and co-evolution of pollinators and angiosperms.

pages.name: Watch: Gymnosperms — Long-Distance Pollination (Ch. 30)
pages.description: Short educational video on gymnosperm life cycle and wind pollination as a key evolutionary breakthrough: gametophyte reduction, male/female cones, fertilization inside the ovule, seed structure, and the ecological transformation enabled by gymnosperm forests.

pages.name: Watch: Reviving the American Forest with the American Chestnut (Ch. 31)
pages.description: TEDxDeExtinction talk by William Powell on American chestnut history, the chestnut blight (Cryphonectria parasitica), oxalic acid mechanism, transgenic restoration using oxalate oxidase, breeding vs. transgenics comparison, and multi-generational restoration strategy.

pages.name: Watch: Wheat Blast — An Emerging Threat to Wheat (Ch. 31)
pages.description: USDA/Kansas State University overview of wheat blast (Magnaporthe oryzae): global food security stakes, disease origins in Brazil, yield losses, U.S. research response including resistance screening and molecular detection tools, and forecasting efforts.

pages.name: Watch: Animal Development & Body Plans (Ch. 32)
pages.description: Animal body plans: symmetry, blastula and gastrulation, blastopore, complete digestive tracts, germ layers, and a classic protostome/deuterostome comparison. Required viewing through 7:20.

pages.name: Watch: Protostomes — A Comparative Overview (Ch. 33)
pages.description: Required short overview of protostome organization: bilateral symmetry, triploblastic development, Lophotrochozoa, Ecdysozoa, lophophores, trochophore larvae, segmentation, molting, and the arthropod body plan.

pages.name: Watch: Arthropods — Body Plan, Exoskeletons & Metamorphosis (Ch. 33)
pages.description: Focused required arthropod viewing: segmentation, tagmata, chitinous exoskeletons, molting, specialized appendages, and metamorphosis.

pages.name: Watch: Animal Organization and Tissues — Part 1 (Ch. 41)
pages.description: Instructor-created Chapter 41 Part 1 overview of levels of organization, epithelial and connective tissues, the three muscle types, neuron structure, myelin, and neuroglia.

pages.name: Watch: Animal Behavior — Why This Toad Is Bad at Jumping (Ch. 53)
pages.description: Crash Course animal-behavior overview with a link to the separate recommended Sapolsky deeper-look page.

pages.name: Watch (Recommended): One Behavior, Many Biological Explanations (Ch. 53)
pages.description: Recommended Robert Sapolsky excerpt connecting sensory cues, neural circuits, hormones, anatomy, development, genes, population differences, and evolution as complementary levels of behavioral explanation.

pages.name: Watch: Population Ecology (Ch. 54)
pages.description: Bozeman Science population-ecology overview with metapopulation and Allee-effect notes plus a link to the separate AP Biology methods and demography page.

pages.name: Watch (Recommended): Measuring Populations, Dispersion, and Demography (Ch. 54)
pages.description: Recommended AP Biology excerpt covering population-estimation methods, mark–recapture, dispersion patterns, human population pyramids, and survivorship curves. Assigned through 9:45.
